1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 084 — **two substantive prover lanes** continuing iter-083's structural advances with revised closure paths; no subagent dispatch this iter per user policy 2026-05-11 "helpers go in the prover's own session").
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-085+, C2 iter-086+, C3 iter-087+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-083 ran two lanes; both made structural progress without regression but neither hit the closure target. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech**: Two `letI` statements added before the L1323 sorry (`letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂`) to surface the transported R-module instances. Iter-083 prover documented **two new findings** (~80-LOC comment block at L1198–1280): (1) `letI` does NOT bridge the `HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ?m` synthesis when the outer ascription is `↑scK₀.X₁` — type-ascription propagates inward during elaboration; (2) `e₁.symm (r • y) = r •_{X₁} e₁.symm y` is NOT rfl despite the `AddEquiv.module` definition, requires `LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply` / `Equiv.smul_def` explicit collapse. **Iter-084 closure route revised**: do NOT pursue path (a) (named-comp on the eqToHom ∘ₗ sum); instead package per-summand R-linearity as a **genuine R-linear map** `Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)`, constructed via `LinearMap.mk` with explicit `map_smul'`. Estimate revised from ~30 LOC to ~50–80 LOC.
21	- **Lane 2 Differentials**: **`cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη` extracted top-level fully closed** at L356–411 (~70 LOC). `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s proof refactored to use `.choose`/`.choose_spec` (not `obtain` — fails inside a `noncomputable def` body because `Exists.casesOn` cannot eliminate into `Type _`). **Three Route 2 attempts on `h_epi` blocked at iter-082 typeclass barrier** (reproduced verbatim): `convert _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective using 1` leaves an iff residual whose RHS requires identifying the descent with `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map` for specific `R,S,B` choices — even with `algebraize`, the bundled `Module` instance comes from `f.hom.toAlgebra` inside `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential`'s internal `letI`-block (which `algebraize`'s freshly synthesised instance does not match). **Iter-084 closure route revised (Option c from iter-083 task report)**: skip the bundled-vs-unbundled bridge entirely; use `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` to show the descent's range contains all `d b` generators (via `Universal.fac` collapse), hence equals ⊤. Split the conjunction `h_exact ∧ h_epi` so `h_epi` closes via Option (c) while `h_exact` remains a single targeted `sorry` (no regression).
22	
23	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
24	
25	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-084 plan |
26	|---|---|---|---|---|
27	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: complete Phase B via path (c) — package `Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)` as a genuine R-linear map; close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1323) via `Φ_j.map_smul r y` + `congr 1`.** |
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: close `h_epi` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L645) via Option (c) — `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` skips the bundled-vs-unbundled bridge; split the `h_exact ∧ h_epi` conjunction so `h_exact` carries the single explicit `sorry` (net 5 → 5).** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-087+) |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
34	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
35	
36	## Current Objectives
37	
38	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
39	
40	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete Phase B via path (c) (genuine R-linear `Φ_j` bundling) to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
41	
42	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1323 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1368 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1397 `h_loc_exact`.
43	
44	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1323) by completing Phase B (per-summand R-linearity) via **path (c)**: package the per-`(i, j)` summand-component as a genuine R-linear map `Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)` constructed by `LinearMap.mk` with explicit `map_smul'`. The closure step is then a direct `Φ_j.map_smul r y` followed by `congr 1` to match `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` on both sides.
45	
46	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These commits are essential prerequisites:
47	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
48	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
49	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3.
50	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1102–1153**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n`; 5-layer `dsimp only`; full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]`.
51	- **The iter-082 S5 prelude at L1161–1170**: `rw [show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl, show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]` re-fold + `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]`. After this prelude, the `j`-projection is adjacent to the comp.
52	- **The iter-083 `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` block at L1207–1208** (surfaces the transported R-module instances).
53	- **The iter-083 ~80-LOC findings comment block at L1198–1280** (records the path (a) blockers + path (c) revised recipe; informs the iter-084 closure approach).
54	
55	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. The per-summand R-linearity at the j-component reduces to: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom` composed with `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r` equals `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r` (`← presheaf.map_comp`); and a ring-hom factoring of the product via `RingHom.map_mul`.
56	
57	**Recipe (path (c) — genuine R-linear `Φ_j` bundling).**
58	
59	- **R0 — Read the post-S5-prelude goal shape.** Use `lean_goal` at L1281 (or wherever the closing context lives after the iter-083 `letI` block) to identify the exact `Σ` form. Expected: `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M_h (e₁.inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M_h (e₁.inv y))` where `M_h := scK₀.f.hom` (if you re-fold via a `let`) OR the explicit alternating-sum form `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom (eqToHom ∘ₗ ∑ i, (-1)^i • Pi.lift …) ((piIsoPi Z₁).symm ·) j`.
60	- **R1 — Construct `Φ_j` per-component.** Define a genuine R-linear map from `∀ i, Z₁ i` to `↑(Z₂ j)`:
61	  ```lean
62	  let Φ_j : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j) :=
63	    { toFun := fun y =>
64	        (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M_h ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))   -- the j-component evaluation
65	      map_add' := by
66	        intro x y
67	        -- Apply additivity through (piIsoPi Z₁).inv, M_h, (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom
68	        simp [map_add]
69	      map_smul' := by
70	        intro r y
71	        -- This is exactly the goal we're trying to prove, expanded out:
72	        -- prove per-summand R-linearity directly via Pi.lift_apply + Finset.sum_apply
73	        -- + Pi.smul_apply (perI₁) + RingHom.map_mul + ← presheaf.map_comp.
74	        <per-summand chain> }
75	  ```
76	  Each summand at fixed `(i, j)` is R-linear because the restriction map `Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)` is an R-algebra hom (chain `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)` via presheaf functoriality, using `algebraMap_naturality` from `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161).
77	- **R2 — Close the goal via `Φ_j.map_smul r y`.** After defining `Φ_j`, the equation `Φ_j.map_smul r y` is *exactly* the desired equation at the j-component (modulo the `congr 1` that matches the outer structure). Apply it:
78	  ```lean
79	  exact Φ_j.map_smul r y
80	  ```
81	- **R3 — If R2 doesn't match syntactically**, use `congr 1` or `change` to reshape the goal to match `Φ_j.map_smul`'s output. The R-action on `↑(Z₂ j)` is via the literal `perI₂ j : Module R (Z₂ j)` from iter-080 — confirm `R-action of Φ_j .codomain` matches.
82	
83	**Partial-credit fallback.** If R1's `map_smul'` proof stalls on a specific per-summand step (e.g. the `RingHom.map_mul` collapse), leave that step as `?_` placeholder and document precisely which step failed. The structural `Φ_j` definition with `LinearMap.mk` and the `map_smul'` placeholder is itself the **substantial iter-084 advance**, even if `h_diff_pi_smul_f` doesn't close in the same session.
84	
85	**Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-083** (do NOT re-attempt verbatim):
86	- `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` to surface R-module instances AND THEN ascription `(r • e₁.symm y : ↑scK₀.X₁)` — fails with `HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ?m` synthesis error (type-ascription propagates inward).
87	- `have ... = ... := rfl` on the smul commutation `r • e₁.symm y = e₁.symm (r • y)` — fails despite `h_mod_X₁`'s `AddEquiv.module` definition (the `apply_symm_apply` collapse is NOT definitional).
88	- `subst hRel` — fails because `n` is the referenced variable inside `prev n + 1`.
89	- `rw [hRel] at *` — creates duplicate variables that break the proof context.
90	- Path (a) `let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := …` named-comp — abandoned. The path was sound in theory but the `k`-linearity barrier (`map_smul` only fires on `k`, not `R`) plus the syntactic non-defeq through `e_i`/`piIsoPi` made it unworkable. **Path (c) (genuine R-linear bundling at the per-component level) supersedes it.**
91	
92	**Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-082** (do NOT re-attempt):
93	- `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` against homogeneous `∘ₗ` — fails (∘ₛₗ-vs-∘ₗ HOU mismatch).
94	- `simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]` — no progress (no explicit `⇑` to fire on).

5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).

105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```

109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/

59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))

1007	      --      `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V) → Γ(W)`), hence `R`-linear.
1008	      --   2. `Pi.map (R-linear) is R-linear` pointwise on each component.
1009	      --   3. Sums of `R`-linear maps are `R`-linear (`LinearMap.add_comp` etc).
1010	      -- The mechanical implementation of these three observations is queued

1016	          e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
1017	            r • e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm y)) := by

1034	        --   `   = (cechComplexFunctor (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)).obj (sheafToPresheaf.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`
1035	        --   `   = (FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (mk _ basicOpenCover ↑s₀).cech).obj P`
1036	        --   `   = (alternatingCofaceMapComplex (ModuleCat k)).obj X'`
1037	        --   where `X' := (FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor (mk _ basicOpenCover ↑s₀).cech).obj P`
1038	        --   and `P := (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
1039	        --
1040	        -- (S3) `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj X` is `CochainComplex.of` with
1041	        --   differential `AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD X m = ∑ i : Fin (m+2), (-1)^i • X.δ i`.
1042	        --
1043	        -- (S4) `X'.δ i = X'.map (SimplexCategory.δ i).op`
1044	        --   `  = (evalOp.obj P).map (((mk _ basicOpenCover ↑s₀).cech.rightOp).map δ_i.op).unop`
1045	        --   With `(evalOp.obj P).map f = Pi.lift (fun i ↦ Pi.π _ (f.unop.f i) ≫ P.map (f.unop.φ i).op)`,
1046	        --   each `X'.δ k` at degree `m` is:
1047	        --   `   Pi.lift (fun (j : Fin (m+2) → ↑s₀) ↦ Pi.π _ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ P.map (φ).op)`
1048	        --   where `φ : ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ((j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) a) ⟶ ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (j a)`
1049	        --   is the appropriate `Pi.map (Pi.lift ...)`-shape morphism in `Scheme.Opens`.

1052	        --   = a ring-hom restriction (since `presheaf.map` produces `CommRingCat` morphisms,

1054	        --

1057	        --   So `(r • y) i = (presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r * y i`.

127	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
128	
129	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
130	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
131	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
132	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
133	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
134	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
135	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
136	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
137	
138	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
139	map of `kToSection`. -/
140	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
143	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
146	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
147	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
148	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
149	  rfl
150	
151	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl
158	
159	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
167	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)

input.new_string:
-- The mechanical execution is ~30 LOC but each step needs careful
        -- elaboration because the deeply nested `Pi.lift` / `Pi.π` / `eqToHom`
        -- structure shifts after every rewrite.  Iter-082+ closure target.
        --
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- ITER-084 advance: clear both iter-083 typeclass barriers.
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- Iter-083 Finding (1) — HSMul source-type ascription barrier — is
        -- resolved by binding the R-module instance under the literal `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)`
        -- form (NOT just `↑scK₀.X_i`).  This makes `HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ?m`
        -- synthesise to the correct target type.  Without this, the type
        -- ascription `(r • e₁.symm y : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁))` propagates `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` as the
        -- SMul source type, which `letI := h_mod_X₁` (typed `Module ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁`)
        -- cannot match by typeclass synthesis (only by defeq via `dsimp`).
        letI hmod_pi_Z₁ : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) := h_mod_X₁
        letI hmod_pi_Z₂ : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := h_mod_X₂
        -- Iter-083 Finding (2) — `e₁.symm (r • y) = r • e₁.symm y` is NOT rfl —
        -- is resolved by an explicit one-step rewrite via `LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply`.
        -- Mechanism: the smul on `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` from `h_mod_X₁ = e₁.toAddEquiv.module R`
        -- is defined as `r • z := e₁.symm (r •_pi e₁ z)` (via `Equiv.smul_def`).
        -- Setting `z := e₁.symm y` gives
        --   `r • e₁.symm y = e₁.symm (r •_pi e₁ (e₁.symm y)) = e₁.symm (r •_pi y)`
        -- after the `e₁.apply_symm_apply` collapse.  The `show ... rw [...]`
        -- below applies this rewrite explicitly.
        rw [show (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv.symm (r • y) =
            (r • (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv.symm y : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)) from by
          show e₁.symm (r • y) = e₁.symm (r • e₁ (e₁.symm y))
          rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]]
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- ITER-084 Phase B residual: genuine per-summand R-linearity.
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- After the two `letI`s and the smul-commutation rw above, BOTH sides of
        -- the goal have the form
        --   `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) <argument>)`
        -- where `<argument>` is `r • e₁.symm y` (LHS) or `e₁.symm y` (RHS, with
        -- an outer `r •`).  The remaining obligation is genuine R-linearity of
        -- the function
        --   `Φ_j : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)`,  `Φ_j z := (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (L z)`
        -- (with `L := eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom`), evaluated at `z = e₁.symm y`.
        --
        -- Per-summand decomposition (S6–S8 from iter-081 + iter-082 chain):
        --   1. Apply `LinearMap.coe_comp` + `Function.comp_apply` to split
        --      `(eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (r • z) = eqToHom (Σ.hom (r • z))`.
        --   2. Use `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (with `perI₁`) on
        --      `Σ.hom (r • z)` to distribute the sum across the smul and reduce
        --      to per-`(i, j)` summands.
        --   3. Per summand at fixed `i`: the summand at `j` reduces (via
        --      `Pi.lift_π` + `Pi.π Z₁ k (e₁.symm z) = z k`) to
        --        `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom ((r • z) (j∘δ_i))
        --           = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom
        --               ((presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i a0} ≤ U).op).hom r * z (j∘δ_i))`
        --      by `Pi.smul_apply` and the `perI₁` definition.
        --   4. Apply `RingHom.map_mul` to split the product, and
        --      `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse the algebra-map chain
        --      `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i a0}) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)`
        --      to `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`, matching `perI₂ j`'s
        --      definition of `r •_{Z₂ j} _`.
        --   5. Reassemble: `Finset.smul_sum` on the RHS, then `Finset.sum_congr rfl`
        --      with the per-summand identity to close.
        -- The mechanics span ~50–80 LOC.  Both iter-083 typeclass barriers are
        -- now cleared (the `letI` block above + the `rw` above), so the remaining
        -- work is genuine R-linearity rather than typeclass scaffolding.
        -- Iter-085+ closure target.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## Status (iter-084)

- **Sorries**: 6 (L502, L826, L854, L1383, L1428, L1457) — net 6 → 6 (no regression, hard cap = 6 maintained).
- **File compiles**: yes, 0 errors. Only style warnings remain (pre-existing — `simp` flexibility, long lines, `show` vs `change`).
- **Axioms (`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`)**: `propext`, `sorryAx`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` — all standard. **No new axioms introduced**.

## h_diff_pi_smul_f (formerly L1323, now ending at L1383)

### Iter-084 advance: BOTH iter-083 typeclass barriers cleared

Two `letI`s + one explicit `rw` inserted between the iter-083 letI block (L1207–1208) and the sorry. After these, the remaining work is **genuine R-linearity content**, not typeclass scaffolding.

#### Attempt 1 — Path (c) bundling at the goal level

- **Approach**: Bundle the per-summand R-linearity as a genuine R-linear map `Φ_j : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[R] ↑(Z₂ j)` via `LinearMap.mk` with explicit `map_smul'`. Apply `Φ_j.map_smul r (e₁.symm y)` to close the main equation.

- **Result**: **PARTIAL** — the two iter-083 typeclass barriers are fully resolved; the residual is the substantive per-summand R-linearity (`Φ_j.map_smul'` content).

- **Key insights** (preserved as in-code comments at L1325–1382):
  1. **Iter-083 Finding (1) resolved**: the HSMul source-type ascription barrier (`failed to synthesize HSMul ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ?m`) is resolved by binding the R-module instance under the **literal `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` form** (not just `↑scK₀.X_i`). The `letI hmod_pi_Z₁ : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) := h_mod_X₁` and `letI hmod_pi_Z₂ : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := h_mod_X₂` instances make typeclass synthesis succeed (verified live via `lean_multi_attempt`).
  2. **Iter-083 Finding (2) resolved**: `e₁.symm (r • y) = r • e₁.symm y` (with `r •` on RHS using `hmod_pi_Z₁`) is closable in one step via `show e₁.symm (r • y) = e₁.symm (r • e₁ (e₁.symm y)); rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]`. Mechanism: the smul on `↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)` from `h_mod_X₁ = e₁.toAddEquiv.module R` is defined as `r • z := e₁.symm (r •_pi e₁ z)` (`Equiv.smul_def`); substituting `z = e₁.symm y` and collapsing via `e₁.apply_symm_apply` gives the desired identity. Verified live: zero diagnostics on this proof block.
  3. **Goal shape after the rw**: BOTH sides have form `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) <argument>)` where `<argument>` is `r • e₁.symm y` (LHS) or `e₁.symm y` (RHS, with outer `r •`). The residual is genuine R-linearity of `z ↦ (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) z)`.

- **Remaining work** (deferred to iter-085+): per-summand decomposition (~50–80 LOC):
  1. `LinearMap.coe_comp` + `Function.comp_apply` to split the comp.
  2. `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (with `perI₁`) to distribute the sum across smul.
  3. Per summand at fixed `i`: use `Pi.lift_π` + `Pi.π Z₁ k (e₁.symm z) = z k` to reduce the j-th component of `Σ.hom (r • z)` to `restr ((r • z)(j∘δ_i))` = `restr ((presheaf.map …).hom r * z (j∘δ_i))`.
  4. `RingHom.map_mul` to split the product; `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse the algebra-map chain to `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`, matching `perI₂ j`'s definition.
  5. `Finset.smul_sum` on RHS + `Finset.sum_congr rfl` to reassemble.

- **Dead-end warning**: 
  - `letI : Module ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁ := h_mod_X₁` (typed at `↑scK₀.X_i`) is **insufficient** for the HSMul synthesis when the outer source is `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` (iter-083 Finding 1). MUST bind at the literal `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` type. The two are defeq via `dsimp`, but typeclass search is syntactic.
  - `simp [Equiv.smul_def]` on the side condition — does NOT fire (`simp` argument unused). Use the explicit `show ... rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]` instead.
  - Naive `rw [map_smul]` on the LHS — fails at the `MulActionHomClass` synthesis (e₁ is `≃ₗ[k]`, not `≃ₗ[R]`).

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.

300	            rw [h1]

696	    -- Mathlib leverage names (all confirmed in iter-072):
697	    --   * `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` (Adjunction/Basic.lean L232)
698	    --   * `Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero` (Adjunction/Additive.lean L66)
699	    --   * `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`
700	    --   * `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` (ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean L53)
701	    --   * `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal'.postcomp_injective`
702	    --     (ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean L101)
703	    --   * `PresheafOfModules.Derivation.d_app` (ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean L63)
704	    --
705	    -- Tactic chain (Steps 1-7 of the strategy):
706	    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
707	    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
708	    -- Goal: `(homEquiv α) ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0` in `Y.Modules`.
709	    -- Unfold α to expose `homEquiv α = ⟨isUniversal'.desc d_target⟩`:
710	    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
711	    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
712	    -- Goal: `⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩ ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0`
713	    --   in `Y.Modules`.
714	    -- Step 4: drop to PresheafOfModules level via `SheafOfModules.hom_ext`, then unfold
715	    -- the `.val` of the pushforward of `β` to expose
716	    -- `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward _).map β.val`.
717	    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
718	    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
719	    -- Re-introduce local names for the ring presheaf morphisms inlined from
720	    -- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, so that the universal-property handle elaborates.
721	    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
722	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
723	        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
724	    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
725	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
726	        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
727	    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
728	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
729	        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
730	    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
731	    -- Step 5: apply `isUniversal'.postcomp_injective`. Two morphisms out of
732	    -- `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'` agree iff their postcompositions with the
733	    -- universal derivation agree as Derivations.
734	    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
735	    -- Goal: equality of two `((pushforward).obj (relativeDifferentials' φ_fg'))`-valued
736	    -- Derivations over `φ_g'`. The RHS is the zero Derivation (postcomp of the zero hom).
737	    -- Step 6: reduce to pointwise via `Derivation` extensionality.
738	    ext U b
739	    -- Step 7: pointwise computation.
740	    --
741	    -- LHS `.d` at `b : Y.presheaf.obj U.unop` reduces, via the postcomp definition and the
742	    -- universal-property fact `(derivation' φ_g').postcomp αv = d_target`, to
743	    --   (β.val.app (f⁻¹ U) .hom) (D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b))
744	    -- where `D_X := derivation' φ_fg'`. By `β`'s universal property

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Ring.Basic
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic

/-!
# The differentials of a morphism in the category of commutative rings

In this file, given a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`,
and `M : ModuleCat B`, we define the type `M.Derivation f` of
derivations with values in `M` relative to `f`.
We also construct the module of differentials
`CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f : ModuleCat B` and the corresponding derivation.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v u

open CategoryTheory

attribute [local instance] IsScalarTower.of_compHom SMulCommClass.of_commMonoid

namespace ModuleCat

variable {A B : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{v} B) (f : A ⟶ B)

/-- The type of derivations with values in a `B`-module `M` relative
to a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
def Derivation : Type _ :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  _root_.Derivation A B M

namespace Derivation

variable {M f}

/-- Constructor for `ModuleCat.Derivation`. -/
def mk (d : B → M) (d_add : ∀ (b b' : B), d (b + b') = d b + d b' := by simp)
    (d_mul : ∀ (b b' : B), d (b * b') = b • d b' + b' • d b := by simp)
    (d_map : ∀ (a : A), d (f a) = 0 := by simp) :
    M.Derivation f :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  { toFun := d
    map_add' := d_add
    map_smul' := fun a b ↦ by
      dsimp
      rw [RingHom.smul_toAlgebra, d_mul, d_map, smul_zero, add_zero]
      rfl
    map_one_eq_zero' := by
      dsimp
      rw [← f.hom.map_one, d_map]
    leibniz' := d_mul }

variable (D : M.Derivation f)

/-- The underlying map `B → M` of a derivation `M.Derivation f` when `M : ModuleCat B`
and `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in `CommRingCat`. -/
def d (b : B) : M :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  _root_.Derivation.toLinearMap D b

@[simp]
lemma d_add (b b' : B) : D.d (b + b') = D.d b + D.d b' := by simp [d]

@[simp]
lemma d_mul (b b' : B) : D.d (b * b') = b • D.d b' + b' • D.d b := by simp [d]

@[simp]
lemma d_map (a : A) : D.d (f a) = 0 :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  D.map_algebraMap a

end Derivation

end ModuleCat

namespace CommRingCat

variable {A B A' B' : CommRingCat.{u}} {f : A ⟶ B} {f' : A' ⟶ B'}
  {g : A ⟶ A'} {g' : B ⟶ B'} (fac : g ≫ f' = f ≫ g')

variable (f) in
/-- The module of differentials of a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
noncomputable def KaehlerDifferential : ModuleCat.{u} B :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  ModuleCat.of B (_root_.KaehlerDifferential A B)

namespace KaehlerDifferential

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
variable (f) in
/-- The (universal) derivation in `(KaehlerDifferential f).Derivation f`
when `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
noncomputable def D : (KaehlerDifferential f).Derivation f :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk
    (fun b ↦ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B b) (by simp) (by simp)
      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B).map_algebraMap

/-- When `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`, this is the
differential map `B → KaehlerDifferential f`. -/
noncomputable abbrev d (b : B) : KaehlerDifferential f := (D f).d b

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[ext]
lemma ext {M : ModuleCat B} {α β : KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M}
    (h : ∀ (b : B), α (d b) = β (d b)) : α = β := by
  rw [← sub_eq_zero]
  have : ⊤ ≤ LinearMap.ker (α - β).hom := by
    rw [← KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation, Submodule.span_le]
    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, LinearMap.mem_ker, ModuleCat.hom_sub, LinearMap.sub_apply, sub_eq_zero]
    apply h
  rw [top_le_iff, LinearMap.ker_eq_top] at this
  ext : 1
  exact this

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The map `KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g').obj (KaehlerDifferential f')`
induced by a commutative square (given by an equality `g ≫ f' = f ≫ g'`)
in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
noncomputable def map :
    KaehlerDifferential f ⟶
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g'.hom).obj (KaehlerDifferential f') :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := f'.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := g.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := g'.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := (g ≫ f').hom.toAlgebra
  have : IsScalarTower A A' B' := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
  have := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' (congrArg Hom.hom fac)
  -- TODO: after https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/19511 we need to hint `(Y := ...)`.
  -- This suggests `restrictScalars` needs to be redesigned.
  ModuleCat.ofHom (Y := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g'.hom).obj (KaehlerDifferential f'))
  { toFun := fun x ↦ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B' x
    map_add' := by simp
    map_smul' := by simp }

@[simp]
lemma map_d (b : B) : map fac (d b) = d (g' b) := by
  algebraize [f.hom, f'.hom, g.hom, g'.hom, f'.hom.comp g.hom]
  have := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' (congrArg Hom.hom fac)
  exact _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D A A' B B' b

end KaehlerDifferential

end CommRingCat

namespace ModuleCat.Derivation

variable {A B : CommRingCat.{u}} {f : A ⟶ B}
  {M : ModuleCat.{u} B} (D : M.Derivation f)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Given `f : A ⟶ B` a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`, `M : ModuleCat B`,
and `D : M.Derivation f`, this is the induced
morphism `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M`. -/
noncomputable def desc : CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M :=
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  ofHom D.liftKaehlerDifferential

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma desc_d (b : B) : D.desc (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d b) = D.d b := by
  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
  apply D.liftKaehlerDifferential_comp_D

end ModuleCat.Derivation
"}
